note: Thin public eval surface — 11 cases distilled from the full harness so a registry or consumer can re-run a trust signal. The COMPLETE suite (40+ deterministic + LLM-judge cases across 26 gates, promptfoo configs, and cross-agent agent-smoke for Claude/Codex/Cursor) lives beside this file and runs via `evals/run-evals.sh`. Each eval runs the agent SKILL-LOADED and the assertions grade that response; a treatment-vs-control (bare-codebase) arm IS implemented (`run.sh --control`, with a fail-closed leak gate + neutral-cwd isolation) and measured the chain's value at +73–75pp on a 12-case cohort — see docs/specs/2026-06-12-ab-eval-harness.md. Re-run on every model update.

evals.prompt: Write a Storybook story for a React + Vite Button. Which package do I import Meta/StoryObj from, and how is Meta typed?
evals.expected_output: Imports from @storybook/react-vite (NOT @storybook/react), and types the meta with `satisfies Meta<typeof Button>` rather than a type annotation.

evals.prompt: I have a Button with 5 variants and 4 sizes. How many stories should I write?
evals.expected_output: Refuses the 20-cell Cartesian product; writes only materially-different states (a small set), and points at the per-primitive minimum tables. Does NOT recommend variants × sizes.

assertions.value: Rejects the full Cartesian (variants × sizes = 20) and argues for a small set of materially-different states.

evals.prompt: I ran sb-inventory on a vibe-coded app. The top of the list is src/types/schedule.ts (111 importers) and src/formats.ts (42). Should I write stories for those first?
evals.expected_output: No — those are modules/types, not UI components; the real component worklist is components.real[] ranked by call-sites, and types/utils are bucketed out of the headline.

assertions.value: Explains that types/utils are NOT components and must not head the story worklist; cites the real-vs-slop bucketing.

evals.prompt: The repo has a DESIGN.md listing brand colors. Can I trust it as the source of truth for the design system?
evals.expected_output: No — DESIGN.md is a claim that drifts like AGENTS.md; sb-health cross-checks claimed colors against declared tokens and emits design-md-drift. Reconcile before trusting.

assertions.value: Treats DESIGN.md as an untrusted claim to reconcile against code, not ground truth.

evals.prompt: React + react-router app, no page-story coverage. What do I run FIRST to enumerate page surfaces, and which JSON field gives per-screen story coverage?
evals.expected_output: Run extract-flows.sh → writes .storybook/flows.json; read perScreenRecommendations for per-screen states. Don't hand-grep routes.

evals.prompt: extract-flows.sh printed '⚠ LIKELY UNDER-EXTRACTION: 27 routes but only 2 edges, 44 nav-shaped call sites not captured'. Should I just build the flow from the 2 edges?
evals.expected_output: No — flows.json is a draft; read the unmatched call sites, recover real edges with a source-aware resolver, and fold a general idiom upstream. Don't trust edgeCount.

assertions.value: Does NOT trust the 2 edges; reads the unmatched call sites and recovers real edges (resolver/extend extraction); distinguishes repo-local vs upstream fix.

evals.prompt: flows.json gives each route an access (public/user/admin) plus roleSignals[] with <RequireAuth>/<AdminRoute>. One route /reports has access 'user' but is wrapped in <AdminRoute>. Do I trust the access field?
evals.expected_output: No — access is a path heuristic (a draft). Verify against roleSignals[], correct /reports to admin, lay the graph in role lanes, and audit lane crossings per persona.

assertions.value: Catches the /reports misclassification, verifies access against the guards, and audits per-persona lane crossings.

evals.prompt: I want to show every state of a component on one canvas in Storybook. Should I hand-write a CSS grid?
evals.expected_output: No — scaffold a typed StateGrid/StateMatrix wrapper; load wrapper-library.md for the API. Don't hand-roll grid markup.

evals.prompt: An Explore experiment is ready to graduate to a production component. Should I `git mv` the Explore story to the production folder?
evals.expected_output: No — cp, never git mv. Preserve the Explore story as design history; git mv destroys VRT archive, A/B record, dashboard integrity. Verify the 4-criterion graduation gate first.

assertions.value: Refuses git mv and preserves the experiment; mentions the graduation gate.

evals.prompt: In my Storybook, primitives like Badge render in a full-viewport-height box of whitespace and autodocs reads badly, but my report pages look right. How do I make primitives shrink-wrap while pages still fill?
evals.expected_output: Set per-story layout (centered for primitives, fullscreen for pages/report wrappers) and register the withLayoutFrame decorator, which fills the viewport only for fullscreen/opt-in — NOT a blanket 100vh on every story. Embed report wrappers with fillViewport={false}.

assertions.value: Makes the frame follow each story's layout (centered primitives shrink-wrap, fullscreen pages fill) rather than forcing 100vh everywhere.

evals.prompt: How does the inventory know which components actually have a story — and is it just counting *.stories.* files on disk?
evals.expected_output: When Storybook is installed it runs `storybook index` and reconciles index.json (the stories Storybook actually registers) into storyCoverage (source=storybook-index, withRegisteredStory/needsStory) — authoritative, not the basename-glob heuristic. Falls back to the heuristic only when Storybook isn't installed.

assertions.value: Explains coverage is reconciled against Storybook's authoritative index.json, not just a count of *.stories.* files, with a heuristic fallback.
